items.standfirst: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

items.title: US, Iran agree on Lebanon ceasefire and lifting sanctions on Iranian oil, but much remains
items.standfirst: Both Tehran and Washington hailed the negotiations that took place in Switzerland on June 22 to create a roadmap to close down the war in the Middle East. US Vice president JD Vance said it laid a good foundation for a deal to end the war. The parties agreed to establish mechanisms to open the strait of Hormuz but it is unclear who will control it.
items.content: Both Tehran and Washington hailed the negotiations that took place in Switzerland on June 22 to create a roadmap to close down the war in the Middle East. US Vice president JD Vance said it laid a good foundation for a deal to end the war. The parties agreed to establish mechanisms to open the strait of Hormuz but it is unclear who will control it.

items.title: Iran agrees to UN nuclear inspectors’ return as part of agreement with US
items.standfirst: Other measures include Washington lifting sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and reopening the strait of HormuzIran has agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country as part of an agreement under which Washington will lift sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and the strait of Hormuz will reopen, the US vice-president, JD Vance, has said.Long-term independent monitoring of Iran’s nuclear programme, which it says is for energy purposes only, was in effect halted last summer after Israel and the US attacked the country. Tehran suspended cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in response to strikes on its nuclear facilities. Continue reading...
items.content: Other measures include Washington lifting sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and reopening the strait of HormuzIran has agreed to allow UN nuclear inspectors back into the country as part of an agreement under which Washington will lift sanctions on Tehran’s oil exports and the strait of Hormuz will reopen, the US vice-president, JD Vance, has said.Long-term independent monitoring of Iran’s nuclear programme, which it says is for energy purposes only, was in effect halted last summer after Israel and the US attacked the country. Tehran suspended cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) in response to strikes on its nuclear facilities. Continue reading...

clusters.title: 10 years after Brexit, Starmer’s resignation highlights Britain’s deeper issues
clusters.neutral_headline: 10 years after Brexit, Starmer’s resignation highlights Britain’s deeper issues
bullet_summary: 10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced that he will resign, paving the way for the country’s 7th leader in a decade
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced he would resign from office after facing a rebellion from members of his own party

items.title: 10 years after Brexit, Starmer’s resignation highlights Britain’s deeper issues
items.standfirst: 10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation.
items.content: 10 years to the day after the Brexit vote, Britain was looking to Andy Burnham as its likely next leader after Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation.

items.title: UK facing 'pivotal moment' as it prepares for 7th PM since Brexit vote
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer won his job after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024. Less than two years later, he resigned as his party rebelled in the wake of widespread losses in local elections. It coincides with the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote, which led to the resignation of Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot and Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi look at how the UK's relationship with the EU will weigh on Starmer's successor, and what led Starmer to resignation.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer won his job after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024. Less than two years later, he resigned as his party rebelled in the wake of widespread losses in local elections. It coincides with the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote, which led to the resignation of Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot and Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi look at how the UK's relationship with the EU will weigh on Starmer's successor, and what led Starmer to resignation.

clusters.title: Second ransom note in Nancy Guthrie case claims she died, sources say
clusters.neutral_headline: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
bullet_summary: Authorities believe two ransom notes addressed to Nancy Guthrie's family — including a note that said she had died — were likely sent by the person or group of people who abducted her
Two people were killed and a child was injured in a shooting inside a library in Chico on Monday, officials said
It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said

items.title: Second ransom note in Nancy Guthrie case claims she died, sources say
items.standfirst: Investigators familiar with the case believe it is likely that two ransom notes sent to Nancy Guthrie's family were written by the person or group that abducted her. While the first note demanded millions in bitcoin, the second claimed that Nancy Guthrie had died, albeit not purposefully, according to sources who reviewed the notes. CBS News' Bradley Blackburn has more details on the case.
items.content: Investigators familiar with the case believe it is likely that two ransom notes sent to Nancy Guthrie's family were written by the person or group that abducted her. CBS News' Bradley Blackburn has more details on the case.

clusters.title: Paris suffocates as deadly heatwave takes hold of the country
clusters.neutral_headline: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
bullet_summary: France experienced its hottest night on June 22 as Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said 40 people -- many of them young -- had drowned since June 18 amid a severe heatwave
France experienced its hottest-ever night Monday into Tuesday as Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said 40 people had drowned across the country since June 18 as they sought to escape the record heat
In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend

items.title: Paris suffocates as deadly heatwave takes hold of the country
items.standfirst: France experienced its hottest night on June 22 as Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said 40 people -- many of them young -- had drowned since June 18 amid a severe heatwave. The deadly heatwave has battered France since last week, disrupting daily life and forcing school closures and train cancellations, with Lecornu convening ministers for a new crisis meeting on June 23 to address the situation. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from Paris.
items.content: France experienced its hottest night on June 22 as Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said 40 people -- many of them young -- had drowned since June 18 amid a severe heatwave. The deadly heatwave has battered France since last week, disrupting daily life and forcing school closures and train cancellations, with Lecornu convening ministers for a new crisis meeting on June 23 to address the situation. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from Paris.

items.title: Drowning deaths soar in France as Europe buckles in peak of heatwave
items.standfirst: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.
items.content: Forty people have drowned in heatwave-related deaths in France since last Thursday, Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu says.

items.title: France issues red alert as heatwave breaks records and death toll mounts
items.standfirst: France experienced its hottest-ever night Monday into Tuesday as Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said 40 people had drowned across the country since June 18 as they sought to escape the record heat. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, the scorching temperatures have led to school closures, cancelled events and disrupted rail services.
items.content: France experienced its hottest-ever night Monday into Tuesday as Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said 40 people had drowned across the country since June 18 as they sought to escape the record heat. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, the scorching temperatures have led to school closures, cancelled events and disrupted rail services.

items.title: France faces historically hot temperatures as heatwave carries on
items.standfirst: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.
items.content: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.

clusters.title: Surfside condo started failing 3 weeks before total collapse, federal report says
clusters.neutral_headline: Deadly collapse at Florida condo happened slowly over several weeks, report finds
bullet_summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) said in the report that two connections between garage columns and the pool deck started to fail around early June
Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Surfside condo started failing 3 weeks before total collapse, federal report says
items.standfirst: A new federal report shows the likely cause of the 2021 Surfside condo collapse in Florida that killed 98 people. According to the report, the structure failures began three weeks before the building fell when two connections between garage columns and the pool deck gave way.
items.content: A new federal report shows the likely cause of the 2021 Surfside condo collapse in Florida that killed 98 people. According to the report, the structure failures began three weeks before the building fell when two connections between garage columns and the pool deck gave way.

items.title: Deadly collapse at Florida condo happened slowly over several weeks, report finds
items.standfirst: Building had been vulnerable before it collapsed in the middle of the night, killing 98 people in 2021The deadly destruction of a Florida beachfront condominium actually started weeks before it collapsed into a pile of rubble in the middle of the night, killing 98 people in 2021 – but the building had been vulnerable from the start, federal investigators found in a final report issued on Monday.The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) said in the report that two connections between garage columns and the pool deck started to fail around early June. The combination of a structure design that did not meet building codes and alterations made to it over its 40 years meant that the other parts of the pool deck weren’t strong enough to withstand the extra load, leading to the type of slow-motion collapse. Continue reading...
items.content: Building had been vulnerable before it collapsed in the middle of the night, killing 98 people in 2021The deadly destruction of a Florida beachfront condominium actually started weeks before it collapsed into a pile of rubble in the middle of the night, killing 98 people in 2021 – but the building had been vulnerable from the start, federal investigators found in a final report issued on Monday.The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) said in the report that two connections between garage columns and the pool deck started to fail around early June. The combination of a structure design that did not meet building codes and alterations made to it over its 40 years meant that the other parts of the pool deck weren’t strong enough to withstand the extra load, leading to the type of slow-motion collapse. Continue reading...

items.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
items.standfirst: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister has told a court he has ordered a halt to preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous stop-work order.Many Kenyans strongly oppose the plan and deadly protests have erupted since the facility was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a large-scale Ebola outbreak. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Protests erupt in Colombia as far-right set to win presidential election
clusters.neutral_headline: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Protests erupt in Colombia as far-right set to win presidential election
items.standfirst: Protests have erupted in Colombia against the election of the far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella. Though election officials have yet to formally announce a winner, with almost all of the votes counted, the pro-Trump lawyer and businessman is set to defeat his left-wing opponent Ivan Cepeda. Trailing behind by around 250 000 votes, the left-wing senator told supporters his team would be challenging results from tens of thousands of voting stations.
items.content: Protests have erupted in Colombia against the election of the far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella. Though election officials have yet to formally announce a winner, with almost all of the votes counted, the pro-Trump lawyer and businessman is set to defeat his left-wing opponent Ivan Cepeda. Trailing behind by around 250 000 votes, the left-wing senator told supporters his team would be challenging results from tens of thousands of voting stations.

items.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
items.standfirst: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityA Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...
items.content: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityA Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election that promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US federal reserve, dies aged 100
clusters.neutral_headline: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US federal reserve, dies aged 100
bullet_summary: The central bank said its former chair “helped establish the credibility that remains one of the Federal Reserve’s most important assets” in a statement on Monday that announced Greenspan’s death
Reported by Guardian Business, Sky News World

items.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US federal reserve, dies aged 100
items.standfirst: Greenspan served under the presidencies of Ronald Reagan, George HW Bush, Bill Clinton and George W BushAlan Greenspan, the influential economist who ​steered US ⁠monetary policy ⁠during ​his ‌five ‌terms as chair ‌of the Federal Reserve ‌under four presidents, ​has died aged 100.The central bank said its former chair “helped establish the credibility that remains one of the Federal Reserve’s most important assets” in a statement on Monday that announced Greenspan’s death. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US Federal Reserve, dies aged 100
items.standfirst: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.
items.content: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.
